content: The Butlers had backed the losing side in the Wars of the Roses , and Ormonde's brother James Butler, 5th Earl of Ormonde had been attainted and executed for treason as a result, but the 6th Earl was quickly pardoned by the new King Edward IV , who greatly admired him
summary: The Butlers had backed the losing side in the Wars of the Roses , and Ormonde's brother James Butler, 5th Earl of Ormonde had been attainted and executed for treason as a result, but the 6th Earl was quickly pardoned by the new King Edward IV , who greatly admired him
